LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. LABOR CODE SECTION 5955 PRECLUDES THE COURT FROM EXERCISING JURISDICTION OVER A CASE THAT CHALLENGE WHAT TRANSPIRED IN A W.C.A.B. PROCEEDING. THAT IS THE ESSENCE OF THE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. PLAINTIFF HAS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION BECAUSE THE DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT PROSECUTED AN ACTION AGAINST PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OBTAINED A FAVORABLE OUTCOME AND DEFENDANTS ARE IMMUNE FROM PROSECUTION. REGAN V PRICE (2005) 131 CAL. APP. 4TH 1491, 1495; GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 821.6, 820.4 AND 820.2. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

calendar.matters:
Motion For Genuiness Of Document;
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Plaintiff Arthur Webb appearing in pro per.

Ruling:
PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB'S Motion For Genuiness Of Document:
No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
PLAINTIFF ARTHUR WEBB'S Motion For Genuiness Of Document is denied. A judgment was entered and served on plaintiff in January 2008. If this is a motion for reconsideration, it is untimely under CCP 657. If it is a motion for a new trial, it is untimely under CCP 659. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/PB)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. LABOR CODE SECTION 5955 PRECLUDES THE COURT FROM EXERCISING JURISDICTION OVER A CASE THAT CHALLENGE WHAT TRANSPIRED IN A W.C.A.B. PROCEEDING. THAT IS THE ESSENCE OF THE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. PLAINTIFF HAS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION BECAUSE THE DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT PROSECUTED AN ACTION AGAINST PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OBTAINED A FAVORABLE OUTCOME AND DEFENDANTS ARE IMMUNE FROM PROSECUTION. REGAN V PRICE (2005) 131 CAL. APP. 4TH 1491, 1495; GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 821.6, 820.4 AND 820.2. (302/PJM/CB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
